warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance
Property: atom–condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic (largest f+) and most nucleophilic (largest f−) sites in phenol.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF  (invoked through chemkit’s fukui-reactivity skill, tier explicitly set via --functional b3lyp and --basis def2-tzvp).
Geometry provenance: single-point population analyses on the user-supplied geometry /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz (no re-optimization performed; calculation is therefore DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model requested).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).  N–1 and N+1 states were computed as doublets (charge +1 and −1, multiplicity 2) for the finite-difference Fukui derivatives.
Software: chemkit v⟂ (backend PySCF) via `chemkit fukui`.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for the neutral, cation, and anion all converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy = true.  The following warnings were issued verbatim by the engine:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live log: the SCF and charge-analysis progress was written live to a `.out` file whose path is recorded in the `cli_invocation` working directory (same stem as the JSON result written to phenol_fukui_dft.json); it can be inspected for full details.

Result (electrophilic site)
The largest electrophilic Fukui index (f+) is 2.1028319 on atom 3 (C, 0-based index 2) of phenol.

Consequently, the headline value reported above is this maximum f+ = 2.1028319 (unitless).
